'''Grumme Vinn''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] and free-trader who operated during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He was the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the ''[[Corsa-Vinn]]'',<ref name="PSG" /> and aided the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] in resisting the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Gamer">{{GamerCite|2|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Duros}}</ref>

==Biography==
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] born into a family of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], Grumme Vinn turned away from his heritage once he was old enough to learn about the dishonest living his parents made in the smuggling trade. Finding no pride in his family, Grumme ran away from home and intended to redeem the Vinn family name by starting up a legal transportation business. He inherited his family's former smuggling vessel, a dilapidated [[Ghtroc Industries/Legends|Ghtroc]] [[720 light freighter/Legends|Class 720 freighter]] named the ''[[Corsa-Vinn]]'', which he flew along the backwater [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].

However, Vinn's intention of running a successful cargo hauling business did not go as planned, and [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of upholding his moral code took their toll, leaving him with a worn and wrinkled appearance. In later years the grumpy [[Captain/Legends|captain]] took whatever legitimate jobs he could find, including hauling supplies to settlements, transporting passengers, and running specialty cargo for picky clients. He generally had little to do with smugglers, preferring his own company, and earned few friends. On several occasions he was almost conned into taking on hidden contraband by crooked drop point agents, but Grumme remained noble despite considering his moral code more of a burden as he grew older.<ref name="PSG" />

==Personality and traits==
Unlike the smugglers in his family and the lawless individuals of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], Grumme Vinn maintained a strong moral code, considering himself a legitimate and noble businessperson. However, he was not an optimistic [[Duros/Legends|Duros]], and many of his attempts at legal business failed by upholding his code, which he came to see as a burden in later years. Vinn refused to consort with smugglers, avoiding them in [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceports]] by preferring to sit alone in a dark corner, and had few friends. He tended to regard everyone with an equal measure of suspicion, although did not carry a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] to protect himself.

In his later years, Grumme Vinn was a sour and grumpy individual, and his physical appearance deteriorated with the pressures of maintaining his moral code. His face became worn and wrinkled, and his wide [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] began [[Color/Legends|greying]] over. He flew the [[Ghtroc Industries/Legends|Ghtroc Industries]] [[720 light freighter/Legends|Class 720 freighter]] ''[[Corsa-Vinn]]'', and typically wore spacer's coveralls and carried a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]].<ref name="PSG" />
